statement.summary: Diffusion-based generative models have reformed generative AI, and have enabled new capabilities in the science domain, for example, generating 3D structures of molecules. Due to the intrinsic problem structure of certain tasks, there is often a symmetry in the system, which identifies objects that can be converted by a group action as equivalent, hence the target distribution is essentially defined on the quotient space with respect to the group. In this work, we establish a formal framework for diffusion modeling on a general quotient space, and apply it to molecular structure generation which follows the special Euclidean group $\text{SE}(3)$ symmetry. The framework reduces the necessity of learning the component corresponding to the group action, hence simplifies learning difficulty ov

proposer: Yixian Xu, Yusong Wang, Shengjie Luo et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper presents a novel theoretical framework (quotient-space diffusion models) with mathematical formalization and molecular generation application. Status is preprint (2026-04-25), not yet peer-reviewed or validated in independent implementations. The core claim—that diffusion modeling on quotient spaces reduces learning complexity for SE(3)-symmetric tasks—is mathematically structured but requires empirical validation and community consensus.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 対称性を除くことで、学習すべき本質的な自由度だけが残る。
